partial_reason: daily_notes table not readable from FUSE sandbox; podcast-derived sections only. The avoid_list is complete and is what the Editor agent most needs.
summary: Listener is in week three of the 4-eps-per-candidate June 23 primary deep-dive arc. Coverage in the last 7 days: Sharif Hidayat (D7 challenger, 5/18), Will Jawando (Exec, 5/19), Vaughn Stewart (D19 House, 5/20), Evan Glass (Exec, 5/21). Today (5/23) the scheduled candidate Sunil Dasgupta is fully done and no new series_episode brief should be produced — system-side bug attempted to queue a deepdive_friedson fallback which was rejected per INVARIANT #1.

Listener's recurring focus this window remains: sanctuary policy on undocumented immigration, Friedson's fiscal-restraint record line-by-line, Wootton High School closure timeline & procedural path, Ben Kramer's full historical record back to 2007, and side-by-side ballot matrix across the listener's actual ballot lines (Exec, At-Large, D7, MD-8, House D19, Senate D19, MCPS-related state-level votes).
themes: Housing supply vs rent stability — Friedson's More Housing N.O.W. vs Jawando's 6% rent cap is now the canonical contrast | Listener wants this distilled into a one-line voter heuristic.

Up-county vs down-county equity — Hidayat's 'red-headed stepchildren' framing for District 7 has been repeated; the Stewart episodes parallel it for District 19 | Useful organizing frame for the listener's MoCo geography

Sanctuary policy stance discovery — Stewart Ep3 explicitly took up the question; Hidayat Ep4 contrasted candidates on sanctuary; still no candidate has been identified who *opposes* the existing sanctuary framework | Listener's open question, restated weekly
Honest-uncertainty framing ('we don't have a public record of X') — being used as a feature of the show, listener has not flagged complaint | Keep but vary phrasing
First-time-candidate vs incumbent framing — Hidayat (challenger, no votes) vs Luedtke (incumbent, votes); explicitly relevant to D7 race
open_questions: Which of the five Exec Democrats actually opposes the existing sanctuary framework (still not answered).
Where do Banerjee and James (the two newcomers to the Exec race) stand on the housing-supply vs rent-cap split — have not been covered yet.
Ben Kramer's full historical voting record back to 2007 (listener priority but Kramer scheduled 5/30; still pending).
Wootton High School Board-vote timeline and the new MCPS rating methodology Rockville-school grades (no candidate has been covered who has a public Wootton position).
Friedson line-item fiscal record beyond More Housing N.O.W. — listener still wants this proven, not just claimed.
underserved_topics: Tier-3 ballot lines (State's Attorney John McCarthy, Sheriff, Clerk, Register of Wills) — not yet covered.
Sixth Judicial Circuit judge race (5 candidates: Burrell, Del Pino, Dietrich, McQueen, Pierre) — not yet covered.
At-Large Council field (17 names incl. Marc Elrich dropping down and BoE President Karla Silvestre) — Dana Gassaway up first on 5/31.
MCPS-specific votes by line item — repeatedly named as listener priority, no episode has done a focused line-item walk.
avoid_list: Opening 'Welcome back to the local politic scan' — used 5/19, 5/20, 5/21; rotate.
Opening 'Good morning, and welcome to the show. Today is <date>, we are <N> days out from the Maryland Democratic primary' — used 5/18, 5/19; rotate the days-out phrasing.
'red-headed stepchildren up here' — Hidayat quote, used in Hidayat Ep3 AND Ep4; do not lead an episode with this again.

'We have not read the memoir for this episode' / 'we are not going to invent' / 'we should not pretend' — honest-uncertainty pattern used heavily 5/19, 5/20, 5/21; keep the substance, rotate the exact wording.

'The June 23 winner effectively wins the seat — District N, like every Council district in Montgomery County, is heavily Democratic in November' — verbatim used 5/18 Ep4; substitute different phrasing for the safe-seat point.
'thirty-six days out' / 'thirty-five days out' / 'thirty-four days out' framing — too repetitive when stacked; vary with date arithmetic phrasing.

Friedson vs Jawando 'supply-side vs rent-cap' setup as the headline housing contrast — used in Jawando Ep4 5/19 AND Stewart Ep3 sanctuary aside 5/20; if used again, frame as 'we said earlier' rather than fresh discovery.
'CROWN Act — Creating a Respectful and Open Workplace for Natural Hair' full acronym expansion — used in Jawando Ep3 5/19; abbreviate on re-mention.
'private K-12 school called the Donoho School' — Stewart-specific, used 5/20; should not recur unless directly relevant.
Episode-opening 'Episode N of four on <name>. Today's title is <title>' — formulaic; rotate sentence structure for variety.
'A method note before we start' / 'A housekeeping note' / 'One thing flagged' — same self-conscious framing recurring across 5/19, 5/20, 5/21 cold opens; consolidate.
Father-was-Mayor-of-Anniston anecdote (Stewart Ep1, 5/20) — used as the surprise hook; do not re-deploy as a hook in Stewart Ep4 if it airs.
next_actions_for_editor: When Banerjee and James episodes air (currently scheduled mid-late June), insist on placing them on the Friedson-vs-Jawando housing axis explicitly so the listener has a 5-way comparison, not just a Friedson-Jawando binary.

When Kramer airs (5/30), enforce the listener-explicit ask: full historical voting record back to 2007, NOT just recent sessions. Dossier brief for Kramer needs to be flagged for early/extra research time.

For the May 23 → May 30 gap (no contested candidate scheduled), consider queuing an office_primer for State's Attorney or the Sixth Circuit judge race instead of letting the legacy deepdive fallback re-fire.
Build a single 'sanctuary positions, ranked' segment once enough candidates have been covered to populate one — the listener has asked this question on every weekly themes pull so far.
